human_verification.notes: Reviewed the package for cultural-program-specific coordination behavior, source quality, ticketing and pricing boundaries, accessibility and security escalation, functionality coverage, and validated spec-pack completeness.

specialist_owned_evidence: Boundary is explicit to cultural-program coordination, booking and run-of-show checks, box-office and front-of-house handoffs, blocker tracking, and closure confirmation rather than generic event execution.

Authority sources cover FTC fee and privacy guidance, DOJ ADA ticket-sales guidance, NIST price-verification standards, Smithsonian cultural-program operating references, AAM ethics guidance, FTC BOTS, PCI DSS, and CISA venue-security guidance.

Evaluation scenarios cover normal in-scope work, missing prerequisites, duplicate ownership, booking or payment mismatches, refund-boundary rejection, safety and fraud escalation, execution-boundary rejection, high-volume intake, low-confidence handling, conflicting-policy escalation, tenant adaptation, and closure confirmation.

The package is organized under catalog/naics-overlays/arts-entertainment-and-recreation/cultural-program-coordination-specialist/ so it remains distinct from adjacent guest-services, front-office, pricing, fraud, and operations lanes.

review_notes: No unreviewed regressions were identified in the local package review.
Functionality coverage is complete in both evaluation/results.json and readiness/evidence.json as required by the issue.

notes: This package is suitable as a validated cultural program coordination specialist for dependency intake, handoff packaging, blocker escalation, booking-aware routing, and closure confirmation.
Runtime remains specification-based until a retained implementation is promoted separately.
